name: Kali

distinctive_features: necklace of skulls or freshly decapitated heads
jewelry made from corpses of infants
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: often depicted standing on the corpse of Shiva

domain_specific_visual.iconography: depicted in a cremation ground or battlefield
domain_specific_visual.avatar_forms: four-armed form and ten-armed Mahakali avatar

functional_attributes.primary_function: goddess of time, death, and destruction

domain_specific_functional.role_in_tantric_traditions: preeminent deity in Hindu tantric and Kalikula worship traditions

relationships.description: Kali is the consort of Shiva, often depicted standing on his corpse.
Kali is a personification of goddess Durga's rage.
Kali defeats the demon Raktabija by consuming his blood.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of divine feminine energy and liberation

contrastive_constraints: NOT a benign or friendly goddess
NOT depicted without her distinctive wild and terrifying features
NOT a generic depiction of a Hindu goddess without specific iconography
